generated_oml:
// C3: Physical sensing components
instance LiDARSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "LiDAR sensor on the vehicle generating raw point cloud data for perception"
    DTDFVocab:producedData LiDARPointCloudTransmission
]

instance MotorSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Motor sensor capturing motor torque and speed from the vehicle"
    DTDFVocab:producedData MotorDataTransmission
]

instance SteeringSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Steering angle sensor measuring the steering input for control"
    DTDFVocab:producedData SteeringDataTransmission
]

instance ServoSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Servo sensor providing position feedback for the steering servo"
    DTDFVocab:producedData ServoDataTransmission
]

instance VehicleStateSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Vehicle state sensor collecting speed and steering angle data"
    DTDFVocab:producedData VehicleStateTransmission
]

// C4: Physical‑to‑virtual interaction (Data transmission)
instance LiDARPointCloudTransmission : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom LiDARSensor
]

instance MotorDataTransmission : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorSensor
]

instance SteeringDataTransmission : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom SteeringSensor
]

instance ServoDataTransmission : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom ServoSensor
]

instance VehicleStateTransmission : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom VehicleStateSensor
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance LiDARPointCloudData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Processed LiDAR point‑cloud data after clustering and tracking"
    DTDFVocab:fromData LiDARPointCloudTransmission
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataTranslationEnabler
]

instance MotorData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Motor torque and speed measurements processed for vehicle control"
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorDataTransmission
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataTranslationEnabler
]

instance SteeringData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Steering angle measurements aligned to common coordinate frame"
    DTDFVocab:fromData SteeringDataTransmission
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataTranslationEnabler
]

instance ServoData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Servo position data converted to standard units"
    DTDFVocab:fromData ServoDataTransmission
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataTranslationEnabler
]

instance VehicleStateData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Raw vehicle state data (speed, steering angle) serialised for the Digital Twin"
    DTDFVocab:fromData VehicleStateTransmission
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataTranslationEnabler
]

// C11: Enablers (tooling & enablers)
instance ROS2MiddlewareEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "ROS2 middleware backbone facilitating ROS2 topic exchanges on the vehicle"
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService, ComputationOffloadingService, RemoteManagementService
]

instance JetPackEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "NVIDIA JetPack 5.0 development kit providing GPU acceleration on the vehicle"
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService, ComputationOffloadingService
]

instance PythonEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Python 3.8 runtime supporting scripting of sensor and data processing nodes"
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService
]

instance CPlusPlusEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "C++ runtime for high‑performance control and perception modules"
    DTDFVocab:enables ComputationOffloadingService, RemoteManagementService
]

instance MATLABSimulinkEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MATLAB/Simulink integration for simulation of vehicle dynamics"
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService
]

instance DataTranslationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Data translation component that serialises raw sensor data, aligns message types, performs unit conversions, and prepares streams for the Digital Twin"
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService, ComputationOffloadingService
]

// C6: Services
instance DataMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real‑time visualization of vehicle state, projected driving path, and detected object tracks, with options to save and generate performance reports"
    DTDFVocab:provides VehicleStateInsight, ObjectTrackInsight, PerformanceReportInsight
]

instance ComputationOffloadingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Server‑side Adaptive Cruise Control logic that detects lead vehicles, computes desired speeds, and streams speed commands to the vehicle"
    DTDFVocab:provides ComputeDesiredSpeedAction, SendSpeedCommandAction
]

instance RemoteManagementService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Remote control interface that allows operators to enable/disable ACC, set target speeds, and trigger emergency braking"
    DTDFVocab:provides EnableACCAction, SetTargetSpeedAction, EmergencyBrakeAction
]

// C17: Insights and Actions
instance VehicleStateInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight into current vehicle speed, steering angle, and state as displayed in the real‑time dashboard"
]

instance ObjectTrackInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight showing tracked moving objects detected from LiDAR point‑cloud clustering on the 2‑D plane"
]

instance PerformanceReportInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight summarizing performance metrics of the vehicle over a selected time window, generated from logged data"
]

instance ComputeDesiredSpeedAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Action to compute desired speed based on ACC logic and lead vehicle detection"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance SendSpeedCommandAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Action to transmit calculated speed commands to the vehicle’s ECU via UDP over Wi‑Fi"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance EnableACCAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Action to enable or disable the cloud‑based Adaptive Cruise Control algorithm"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance SetTargetSpeedAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Action allowing operators to set a desired target speed for the vehicle"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance EmergencyBrakeAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Action to trigger an emergency braking sequence remotely from the operator interface"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "Control commands originate from the User Interface (Control Panel) and include enabling/disabling adaptive cruise control (ACC), adjusting target speed, or issuing an emergency brake. These commands are transmitted to the Actuation FE, which converts desired speed values into motor‑voltage signals and steering commands for the servo. The actuation data are then passed through the data‑translation component, which serialises and aligns them before the command is sent over the Wi‑Fi/UDP link to the physical actuation hardware. Feedback mechanisms, conflict resolution logic and command‑execution confirmation are not described in the provided documents."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system operates with a temporal granularity of 100 ms, matching the required clock‑synchronisation accuracy mandated by safety‑critical vehicular standards and constrained by hardware limits. Devices share a unified notion of time to enable computation offloading, with inter‑component synchronization maintained within a 100 ms window to meet latency and timing requirements."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The overall orchestration aligns with ISO 23247 Reference Architecture, mapping software components to functional entities and implementing each FE as an individual ROS 2 package containing one or more ROS 2 nodes. The architecture pattern is entity‑based, with data management and control mechanisms grouped under the Digital Twin Entity, physical device functionalities under the Physical Twin Entity, and user‑facing components under the User Entity. Resource management is achieved through ROS 2’s node and package mechanisms, and system‑wide coordination is handled via inter‑entity messaging and shared time‑stamped data streams."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin’s technical network uses ROS 2 for intra‑component messaging (implying DDS as the underlying transport) and relies on a Wi‑Fi network for inter‑entity communication via UDP. The system must maintain a network latency of no more than 100 ms between the Digital Twin and the Physical Twin Entity. Bandwidth, reliability, redundancy and security protocols (e.g., TLS, authentication) are not detailed in the provided documents."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The system is deployed across multiple hardware platforms: the Point‑Cloud Clustering & Actuation components reside on the Physical Twin Entity, while data‑collecting, reporting, and storage components run on a separate device external to the vehicle. Ego‑vehicle state data are stored on this external storage device. Specific hosting environments (cloud, edge, on‑premise), computational or storage capacities, scalability mechanisms, deployment models, and availability guarantees are not specified in the provided documents."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The ISO 23247 Part 2 reference architecture specifies an entity‑based framework for Digital Twin development in manufacturing.  It defines four core entities: the Observable Manufacturing Element (OME) which represents the physical asset; the Data Collection and Device Control (DDC) Entity responsible for ingesting sensor streams and actuating the OME; the Digital Twin (DT) Entity that holds the virtual model and analytics; and the User Entity that offers monitoring and command interfaces.  Each entity is decomposed into Functional Entities (FEs) which encapsulate modular tasks such as data transformation, user‑interface rendering, or device control.  The standard references OPC Unified Architecture (OPC UA) as a concrete implementation example for communication, but does not prescribe any proprietary stack.  It also requires that any deployed DT framework be capable of exchanging data, model state, and control commands in compliance with the ISO 23247 data‑exchange specifications."
]
